comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: AUTONOMOUS BUILD COMPLETE — ticket LEFT OPEN for human browser validation per Joe's direction. Built + independently security-reviewed: rebar workflow edit <file> serves a bpmn-js host (standard Modeler, BPMN-only palette -> constrained metamodel) over the 00da serializer; Save round-trips BPMN->IR (un-mappable/invalid edits rejected, file untouched; prior IR backed up to .bak; visual format never committed). Out-of-process (bpmn-js CDN browser-side; Python is stdlib http.server + serializer). Security hardened: loopback-only + per-session token + Host-header check on /save (CSRF/DNS-rebinding); XXE blocked. 9 offline tests cover launcher/server/round-trip/rejection/CSRF/backup/XXE. Commit 8b16a0ee. REMAINING (the epic gate Joe will validate): open the editor in a real browser, edit flow/steps/loops/conditionals visually, Save, and confirm the IR round-trips as expected.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Deliver the first-order requirement: humans view AND edit workflows visually (change flow, add/remove/modify
steps, loops, conditionals) without committing any visual artifact.

## What
Embed bpmn-js as an out-of-process edit-time tool. Provide a command/flow that loads IR->BPMN, opens the editor,
and round-trips edits back to the IR (reusing the serializer). Editing is constrained to the BPMN metamodel (no
un-mappable shapes — unlike a free-form canvas). The visual format is NEVER committed.

## Scope
- an edit-time launcher (CLI/MCP) + a bpmn-js host (browser / electron / vscode-webview)
Out: the serializer (separate story).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A human can open a workflow IR in bpmn-js, edit flow/steps/loops/conditionals, and save back to the IR.
- [ ] Edits are constrained to the BPMN metamodel (cannot produce un-mappable shapes); the visual format is not committed.
- [ ] Edit-time dependencies are out-of-process; the runtime/client surface is unaffected.

file_impact.reason: bpmn-js host + loopback edit server + BPMN->IR save round-trip

compiled_state.title: Ephemeral bpmn-js visual editing
